title: BSNC Welcomes 2024 Summer Interns
summary: Please welcome BSNC’s 2024 interns! This group of 15 is the largest BSNC’s had so far, including Shane Agnaboogok, Nathaniel Appoloni, Jorden Barnhill, Joseph Butzke, Aiyana Campbell, Kyle Collins, Jessica Davis, Brady Dickens, Avery Hansell, Reece Ivanoff, Adria Lawrence, Page Miller, Isaiah Nayokpuk, Luke Polito and Taylor Tiulana. Shane Angnaboogok I am Shane Angnaboogok, my Inupiaq name is Kokituk, I was born and raised in Fairbanks, but I’m originally from Shishmaref and Wales. My dad is Dallas Ningealook from Shishmaref, and my mom is Vernae Angnaboogok from Wales. My grandparents are Daniel and Evelyn Olanna from Shishmaref, and my grandma is Edith Pinson from Wales. I am currently attending the University of Alaska Fairbanks (UAF) to pursue my bachelor’s degree in business administration with a concentration in fi...

title: Youth Development & Culture Grants Now Open
summary: RurAL CAP anticipates funding 6-12 projects of up to $6,000 to $16,000 per year, with no match required . The purpose of the Youth Development and Culture Grant Program is to reduce the disproportionate number of minority youth who come into contact with the juvenile justice system. Alaska Natives m...

title: Alaska SBDC Awarded $3 Million in SBA Grants to Empower Alaska Businesses, Healthcare Innovation
summary: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE: May 28, 2024 Alaska, May 28, 2024 – The Alaska Small Business Development Center (SBDC) is excited to announce it has been awarded a combined $3 million in new grant funding, thanks to actions taken by Senator Lisa Murkowski (R-AK). title: NATO has always been an Arctic Alliance (Part I)
summary: According to historical documents, the Arctic has figured into NATO security from the earliest days of the Alliance’s existence. The post NATO has always been an Arctic Alliance (Part I) appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .
